text.title: App Router file conventions and project layout
text.summary: The App Router maps filesystem paths under app/ to URL routes; each segment can define page, layout, loading, error, and not-found files.

rule.statement: Place exactly one file per reserved name per route segment: page.tsx (route UI), layout.tsx (persistent shell), loading.tsx (streaming skeleton), error.tsx (error boundary, must be 'use client'), not-found.tsx (404), global-error.tsx (root error boundary).
rule.rationale: Missing loading.tsx causes the entire layout tree to block on navigation. Missing error.tsx lets exceptions bubble to the nearest parent boundary.

rule.statement: Wrap a folder in parentheses — (group-name) — to share a layout without adding a URL segment.
rule.rationale: Avoids duplicating providers (auth wrappers, theme) across logically separate sections like (auth) and (marketing).

example.code: app/ ├── (marketing)/ │ ├── layout.tsx # marketing layout, no /marketing in URL │ ├── about/page.tsx # /about │ └── contact/page.tsx # /contact ├── (auth)/ │ ├── layout.tsx # centered auth layout │ ├── login/page.tsx # /login │ └── register/page.tsx # /register └── dashboard/ ├── layout.tsx └── page.tsx # /dashboard

rule.statement: Name dynamic folders with square brackets: [id]. Access the segment value via the params prop. In Next.js 15 params is a Promise — always await it.
rule.rationale: Next.js 15 made params async; synchronous access (params.id) silently returns undefined in builds targeting the new runtime.

example.note: Next.js 13/14 pattern — breaks silently in Next.js 15

rule.statement: Use @slot folders for parallel routes (multiple pages in one layout). Use (.)path or (..)path prefixes for intercepted routes (modal patterns that show a page without full navigation).
rule.rationale: Parallel routes allow dashboards with independent loading states per panel. Intercepted routes enable photo/modal patterns where the URL changes but layout stays.

rule.statement: Create route.ts (not page.tsx) for API endpoints. Export named HTTP method functions (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE). Only create route.ts for webhooks or third-party integrations — internal data access goes through Server Components or Server Actions.
rule.rationale: Routing data through an API route from a Server Component adds an unnecessary HTTP round-trip; direct DB access in Server Components is faster and simpler.

text.reference: Root layout with metadata, font, and Providers
Dynamic page with generateStaticParams and generateMetadata
route.ts with GET and POST handlers
